title: TxBuildError: typed error enum for transaction building failures
description:
Define a typed error enum for all transaction building failure modes. This must be a standalone module (wallet/src/tx_error.rs) with no dependencies on zcash_primitives so the error types are stable even as the builder evolves.

pub enum TxBuildError {
    InsufficientFunds { have: u64, need: u64 },  -- have < need (after fees)
    DustAmount { amount: u64, threshold: u64 },   -- amount < DUST_THRESHOLD
    NoSpendableNotes,                              -- wallet has notes but none have plaintext columns
    AnchorMismatch,                                -- notes from different anchor heights mixed
    InvalidRecipient(String),                      -- address string failed to parse
    UnsupportedAddressType(String),                -- transparent/unknown address type
    AmountOverflow,                                -- target + fee > u64::MAX
    BuilderError(String),                          -- zcash_primitives builder returned error
    ParamsNotLoaded(String),                       -- Sapling params file missing or corrupt
    WitnessDeserialize(String),                    -- witness_data bytes could not be parsed
    NoteDeserialize(String),                       -- note plaintext bytes could not be parsed
}

Implement Display for each variant with an actionable message (a user seeing this at 2am must understand what to do).
Implement std::error::Error.
Never use generic 'something went wrong' messages — each variant must name the specific fault.

Tests:
- Test Display output for each variant (not just 'not empty' — assert the key noun is present)
- Test that each variant is constructable (ensures exhaustive coverage)

Use a research subagent to check if anyhow::Error or thiserror is already in wallet/Cargo.toml before deciding which derive macro to use.
acceptance_criteria: TxBuildError compiles; Display test for each variant passes; no variant says 'error' without naming what errored
